question: What are the three parameter modes IN, OUT, and IN OUT?
options: IN passes a value in (read-only inside); OUT returns a value out (starts as NULL inside); IN OUT passes a value in and returns a possibly-modified value

explanation.uz: IN — qiymatni ichkariga uzatadi (ichida faqat o'qiladi). OUT — natijani qaytaradi (ichida boshda NULL). IN OUT — qiymatni ichkariga uzatib, o'zgartirilgan qiymatni orqaga qaytaradi.
explanation.en: IN passes a value in (read-only inside). OUT returns a value out (NULL inside at first). IN OUT passes a value in and returns a possibly-modified value.

question: What does the NOCOPY hint on an OUT / IN OUT parameter do?
options: It copies the value twice for safety
It asks Oracle to pass the parameter by reference instead of by value (copy) — faster for large collections/records, but on an unhandled exception the actual parameter may be left partially modified

explanation.uz: Odatda OUT/IN OUT qiymat nusxasi bilan uzatiladi (copy-in/copy-out). NOCOPY — havola (by reference) orqali uzatishni so'raydi: katta kolleksiya/record uchun tez. Kamchiligi: ushlanmagan istisnoda haqiqiy parametr qisman o'zgargan holda qolishi mumkin.
explanation.en: Normally OUT/IN OUT are passed by value (copy-in/copy-out). NOCOPY asks to pass by reference: faster for large collections/records. Downside: on an unhandled exception, the actual parameter may be left partially modified.

question: Definer's rights (the default) vs Invoker's rights (AUTHID CURRENT_USER)?
options: Definer's rights: the subprogram runs with the privileges and schema of its OWNER (default). Invoker's rights: it runs with the privileges and schema of whoever CALLS it
Definer's rights uses the caller's schema

explanation.uz: Definer's rights (standart) — subprogram egasining huquqlari va sxemasi bilan ishlaydi. Invoker's rights (AUTHID CURRENT_USER) — chaqiruvchining huquqlari va sxemasi bilan ishlaydi (masalan har foydalanuvchi o'z jadvaliga ishlashi uchun).
explanation.en: Definer's rights (default): the subprogram runs with the owner's privileges and schema. Invoker's rights (AUTHID CURRENT_USER): it runs with the caller's privileges and schema (e.g. so each user works against their own tables).

question: Why is putting a COMMIT inside a function/procedure that is called within a larger transaction usually a bad idea?
options: It makes the call run faster

It breaks the caller's atomicity — the caller can no longer roll back the whole unit of work as one transaction; commit/rollback should be controlled at the top level (or use an autonomous transaction if truly independent)
It is required for the function to return

explanation.uz: Chaqirilgan subprogram ichidagi COMMIT chaqiruvchining atomarligini buzadi — endi butun ishni bitta tranzaksiya sifatida ROLLBACK qilib bo'lmaydi. Commit/rollback yuqori (chaqiruvchi) darajada boshqarilishi kerak; chinakam mustaqil bo'lsa — autonomous transaction ishlatiladi.
explanation.en: A COMMIT inside a called subprogram breaks the caller's atomicity — the whole unit of work can no longer be rolled back as one transaction. Commit/rollback should be controlled at the top level; if the work is truly independent, use an autonomous transaction.
